PageResearchNote.description:
LLM output for per-page comprehension during agentic search.

Each page read during the search loop is summarized into a research note
capturing key findings, perspectives covered, and knowledge contribution.

summary.description: 150-200 word research summary focused on findings relevant to query
summary.title: Summary

perspectives.description: STORM perspectives covered by this page
perspectives.title: Perspectives

key_facts.description: 3-5 specific facts/data points with numbers, dates, or named entities
key_facts.title: Key Facts

knowledge_contribution.description: What new understanding this page adds to the research
knowledge_contribution.title: Knowledge Contribution

PageResearchNote.title: PageResearchNote

schema_json.description: Batch wrapper for page research notes with invalid-note filtering.

notes.description: Research notes for each page read
notes.title: Notes

schema_json.title: PageSummaryBatch

prompt:
You are a research analyst reading web pages for a deep research project.

RESEARCH QUESTION: How does damp heat aging affect the adhesion strength of DVS-PEI encapsulants in flexible electronics?

Read the following 5 pages and create a research note for each.

--- PAGE: Influence of environmental conditions on dentin bond strengths of ... ---
URL: https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/15470878/
CONTENT:
Influence of environmental conditions on dentin bond strengths of one-application adhesive systems - PMID: 15470878 Influence of environmental conditions on dentin bond strengths of one-application adhesive systems Abstract Resin composites are considered susceptible to environmental conditions that might affect bond strength. This study investigated the influence of relative humidity and temperature on the dentin bond strength of newly developed one-application adhesive systems. Bonding systems employed in this study were five commercial one-application adhesive systems. Labial surfaces of bovine incisors were ground wet on 600-grit SiC paper. The teeth were transferred to a controlled temperature and humidity chamber and the specimens were prepared in six different environmental conditions, A) 25 +/- 0.5 degrees C, 50 +/- 5% RH, B) 25 +/- 0.5 degrees C, 80 +/- 5% RH, C) 25 +/- 0.5 degrees C, 95 +/- 5% RH, D) 37 +/- 0.5 degrees C, 50 +/- 5% RH, E) 37 +/- 0.5 degrees C, 80 +/- 5% RH, F) 37 +/- 0.5 degrees C, 95 +/- 5% RH. The dentin surfaces were treated according to each manufacturer's instructions. Resin composites of each bonding system were condensed into a mold and light irradiated. After storage in 37 degrees C water for 24 hours, 10 specimens per group were tested in a shear mode in a testing machine at a crosshead speed of 1.0 mm/minute. Statistical analysis was carried out with one-way ANOVA followed by Duncan's multiple range tests at a p-value of 0.05. The dentin bond strengths of one-application adhesive systems decreased with increasing RH. These data suggest that the relative high humidity in the oral environment needs consideration in developing clinical procedures for management of these one-application adhesive systems. Publication types MeSH terms Substances LinkOut - more resources Miscellaneous

All Rights Reserved iii To my brother , for being my greatest champion. iv ## ACKNOWLEDGMENTS First and foremost, I extend my sincere gratitude to Dr. Yaghoob (Amir) Farnam, my academic advisor at Drexel University, for his unwavering guidance, support, and mentorship throughout my Ph.D. studies. His unique forward -thinking perspective, and visionary outlook towards cementitious materials research not only nourished my knowledge, but also played a pivotal factor during this research endeavor. Equally, I am grateful to all my Ph.D. dissertation committee members , Dr. Miguel Pando, Dr. Franco Montalto, Dr. Grace Hsuan, and Dr. Ahmad Najafi for providing valuable insights throughout my academic journey, which facilitated generation of further research scopes and enrichment of my thesis. Furthermore, I would like to acknowledge Compass Minerals for providing the financial support , which was instrumental in acquiring necessary materials and equipment to carry out this research. My sincere appreciation also goes to Drexel College of Engineering, Drexel University facilities, and the Department of Civil, Environmental Engineering for their technical support and recognition during this journey . Moreover, I incredibly appreciate for the camaraderie and continuous support of my friends and colleagues: Mohmmad Houshmand, Parsa Namaki Araghi, Nishant Shrestha, Kham Phan, Mohamed Cissao, Priscilla Kirabo, Anas Tatouli, Victor Onukwugha , Yousif Alqenai, and Sharaniya Visvalingam , Md Irfan Iqbal, Jialuo He, and Geetika Mishra . Their unwavering support during my academic journey at Drexel University has helped me undertake such a daunting task, especially with the construction of the large -scale concrete slabs at the outdoor location. v Finally, I would like to acknowledge my parents , for being steadfast in support and providing unconditional lov e, nurture, and guidance which have a consistent source of strength . Special acknowledgement to my brother, Ruhit, who is an outstanding genius. To m y wife, Antara, your presence in my life has been a beacon of encouragement . You deserve my most sincere gratitude ; you have always pushed me to strive for excellence, look beyond limitations, and reach new heights. vi ## TABLE OF CONTENTS LIST OF TABLES ................................ ................................ ................................ ...................... x LIST OF FIGURES ................................ ................................ ................................ ................... xi LIST OF NOMENCLATURE ................................ ................................ ................................ . xvi ABSTRACT ................................ ................................ ................................ ........................... xvii 1. --- PAGE: The Use of Warm Air for Solvent Evaporation in Adhesive Dentistry ---
URL: https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC10219392/
CONTENT:
Abstract Any excess solvent from dental adhesive systems must be eliminated prior to material photopolymerization. For this purpose, numerous approaches have been proposed, including the use of a warm air stream. This study aimed to investigate the effect of different temperatures of warm air blowing used for solvent evaporation on the bond strength of resin-based materials to dental and nondental substrates. Two different reviewers screened the literature in diverse electronic databases. In vitro studies recording the effect of warm air blowing to evaporate solvents of adhesive systems on the bond strength of resin-based materials to direct and indirect substrates were included. A total of 6626 articles were retrieved from all databases. From this, 28 articles were included in the qualitative analysis, and 27 remained for the quantitative analysis. The results of the meta-analysis for etch-and-rinse adhesives revealed that the use of warm air for solvent evaporation was statistically significantly higher (p = 0.005). For self-etch adhesives and silane-based materials, this effect was observed too (p 3.0.CO;2-A. [DOI] [PubMed] [Google Scholar] - 64.Pashley D.H., Carvalho R.M., Tay F.R., Agee K.A., Lee K.W.
